body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

transcript.text: Dr. April. God bless [Music] Yeah, we will wait a few more minutes. But before we get started anyway, can we just make sure that anyone that's in the room is logged in? Okay. Hi everyone. Welcome to suit for IATF123. >> Could someone close the door, please? >> Thank you. Okay, so this is the note well. Um, you've probably seen this already a few times. It's important to keep in mind that everything we say and do in this meeting is part of the IATF process. You should have already read it and be clear of what it has and means. Uh, this is the note really well. It's about making sure that everyone is respectful on how we interact both in person and online. Um a reminder this session is being recorded and again just make sure that anyone in the room is logged in. We do already have some note takers. So thank you so much Okay. Um this is the agenda for today. Um we have two um AD evaluation for the SU report and update management and um approved for the announcement sent is for S MTI and trust domain and three more on editors Q the S manifest s firmware encryption s mud um any bajin um um ain bashing any other topics would like to One, two. Yeah. Okay. Okay. Then we will start from the first one will be the suit report. Um, next page is clicking. Okay. Um, this is the few days ago had the last update suit report 14. Um, it's in the ADA evaluation and the ne next um draft is the suit manifest. Oh no, it's suit update management. Sorry. Um, this one is um yes from from the it's before the ITF123 start. It's also an AD evaluation. And uh and the next one is suit MTI. The title have changed to cryptographic algorithm for internet of things devices from um which used to be called mandatory to implement. Um this one is updated I think it was yesterday or a few days ago and that's MTI 23 it's approved announcement sent and the next one is the suit trust domains it's 12 um and that's this is also um just updated and this is also approved for an announcement sent and this is the first pages uh most of the changes we had from interim last interim Yes. >> So, just to be clear of what my plan is. So, I'm Debb Koulie, your responsible AD. Um, I will look at the changes that have been made to suit report and uh if those are good, then I will progress that to ITF last call. Um, after I do that, I have I actually have a couple of other things I need to look at, but then I'm going to do the suit update management and and read that and see. And I expect I'll have comments on that because that's the way that works. Um, but suit report I'll do probably before middle of August. Um, and see where we get. So, that's my plan. Is that okay? >> I mean, not like you get to choose, but That seems reasonable. >> Yeah, that's very thank you. >> Okay, thank you. >> And these are the one um and the RF RFC editor Q. So the S manifest 35 uh 34, it was updated just before S um interim last time. Um and uh and the suit firmware encryption. This had a little bit of editorial update but the content is not changed. and uh suit s suit um strong assertant of IoT network access requirements s mud it's uh yes this is um I think was as it is as a s um I122 and uh in the last I added uh um how to do the cdddddio syntax check in the s report uh git repository so I would I would like to um um yes introduce that at the at the time if in any other business time any yes anything else okay then the first one will be oh yeah I think I need to I'm the person think Oops. >> Yes. So for all these drafts sitting in the editor's queue, right, you're going to get you're going to get pinged on by the editor and by maybe Ayanna. So the mud draft was pinged on by Ayanna. You need to keep an eye out. The quicker you reply to those messages, the quicker things move along. You're at a part of the process for all these drafts where things take a little bit of time. But um it doesn't help to have the authors wait like three weeks to reply to an editor to really sort of slow the process down a lot. So um both the mud draft and the manifest draft I think I've had Ayanna comments recently um as things move along because a lot of this is in misref because they're waiting on the MTI draft primarily I think. So um you just need to be you need to be keeping track like you need to be checking to see whether you're getting editor editor comments and when you do that you need to reply there are some of those editor comments that have to be replied by every author not just one author. So all of you hangers on I mean I didn't say that all of your people who don't normally reply to messages are going to have to reply to messages because it won't be published unless you all reply. So get in the habit. >> Thank you. >> First slide report. >> Um is it possible to consider? >> Yeah, sometimes. Oh yeah, there it is. Hello. So the first one is suit report. Brendan, are you there? >> I am there. >> Okay. >> I have just passed you slide control as well. >> All right. Let's uh start with a little bit of a status update. Um so uh the last status that we had before was that there were some ad comments. Thanks again for those Deb. Um, we've now responded to all of those and uh published a new draft. Uh, it's now up to version 14. Uh, so let's just quickly go through a summary of the changes. One of the uh, ballot position comments we got was that it would be really nice to have a summary of the changes since last call. So, I've incorporated that into this as well. So, this one's relatively straightforward. the there weren't a lot of changes since IETF122. Sorry, there weren't a lot of changes before IETF122 since the last call. So, this actually works out to both the changes since 122 and the changes since working group last call. Um, so it's mostly just clarifications, um, consistency, references, that sort of thing. But we have added uh a more substantial section on expert review instructions, a media type registration template, and we've substantially improved the security consideration section. So, I I hope that that will cover the remaining commentary that we received. Wait, sorry, this isn't um this isn't a ballot position comment. This is a Deb's comment. Sorry, Deb. U got lost between my two drafts here. Uh yeah. So, so this is to hopefully to address the comments that uh Deb has made and this should be pretty well there now, I think, up until we start getting ballots on it, of course. Um so, we've got a few steps to come. Um we've got a number of comments uh that have been replied to. So, I guess uh we're waiting for a follow-up from Deb, but there is going to be need to be a another uh copy of this draft issued because somehow through the magic of uh of a variety of git changes, we have wound up with a uh anformational draft that really should be a standard. Um I'm not sure how that happened. It's fascinating. It looks like that hasn't changed at all ever. And yet at the same time, there are definitely versions of this draft which have been listed as standard. So that's that's to come. Or did I already publish it? It's a bit lost now. Anyway, uh that's it for that one. Um so if there's any comments. Hi Deb. So you have a media type registration in this? >> Yes, >> we need to submit that. >> So we need to submit the media type registration um before we put it into ITF last call because media type registrations take a while and um it's best to have them started as early as possible. So there is information around and about that says how to do that. I'll look that up and send it to you if you don't find it before I do. Um, >> okay. >> Because that has to be that has to be done. And what happens is normally you put the media type registration in and the media type people sort of say and then I poke the ads that are responsible for media type registrations and they make them do it. So that's why it's sort of a long poll. It's in it's one of the questions I ask when I have to issue the ballot for this is is there a media type registration and has it been submitted. So the earlier we do this the better off it is. Um the other person that's really good at this is Thomas who I don't see here but he does know how to do that as well. >> Okay. >> Do you yeah >> there you go. um >> what they need to do to follow it. >> And as you say, you have to nag them. >> You do have to nag them. And it is better for them to do it now, right? Early early. Early and often like voting. >> A really Okay. Um, the next one is the next slide. Was it? >> Oh, is the update manage? Okay. Okay. >> Okay. Um I will be temporary become a author of the draft. Yes. And okay. Um so this is the changes from the feature wise is in from the suit in inter June 11. Um the title changed from um crypto algorithm recommendation for suit uh software update of internet of things which is a suit to crypto algorithm for internet internet of things device which is more generic titles and these are the all uh um comments from um um I give uh uh reviews I think and uh and used to have the profile um separate with current and future um listed. The only one future was the um specifying for the Q PQC algorithm and then um we change it to everything in flat uh as the same all profile level instead of um separating classical one and PQC and yes so this is some um security considerate portion what we updated it's been um um discussed in uh many ITF and the mailing list is um so just more a clarification so payload encryption protection is uh uh for the sensitive content inside uh um the firmware and if you when it's doing the distribution on the internet or USB or some other method and it doesn't mean that uh this um encryption will prevent when the device itself is uh compromised so or if some some of the firmware is written in the place which is not encrypted that's often happened in the um commodity devices. Okay. Um next page next page is me and uh okay and this is this is a really uh good operational consideration for uh when you use the suit MTI plus suit uh u manifest um especially for the implementing the um the devices with the based on the suit manifest MMDI. So um guidance of the how to discover supported profile on the target device because the target device may not always have the all the uh profile it's listed to suit MTI and it might and then need to wait find a way to select it and also um it might require to changing the profile in the um in the s of um the the different profile from the first selection. So how it's uh describing what when to and um and that could happen. And so also when doing the search f um firmware update or any of the s um suit manifest operation on the device, it's always um please consider it's logging and reporting back the result is the recommendation. and al and this is uh the um for it's really for me was really um good information for the HSS and LMS PQC uh crypto for um what what is the way to use the size of the signature for the verification and the the uh the trees the the height height of the trees And uh and another thing is the um there's a five profiles which is the developer or creator or generator or author of the suit uh uh suit um um suit binary is mandatory to implement or to have the capability to um uh handle the crypto and in the profiles and however the rece receiver who is going to conduct the update or process the uh pro uh profile only um mandatory is oh no it's oh it's not mandatory for the um um PQC HSS LMS it's recommended and uh another thing is was the coana assignment few uh ES256 and EDSA became um deprecated so at had to change the um number and update ated the um draft and the CDDF file. This is also affecting to the T protocol draft. So which um is which is going to be probably need to update too and yes so these are editorials um changes was there was many um for example with suddenly in the draft they was using many abbreviations so I have added explanation and yes um added the relationship with the suit manifest and it's also the consideration um chapter and this this um consideration chart was the one of the portion we changed the huge many of the line is one of the reason was uh exceeding the 72 character um with uh uh error or warning from the um knit nit tools I forgot >> ID >> ID net ID net tools yes which is manually it's difficult to change it so we we we open up the chart as a just a listing in the draft I think next page no that's that's the final okay comment from the room or online. Brandon. >> Okay. >> Yes. Okay. So, next one is S trust domains. Yes. >> Um, yeah, sorry. I I did um raise my hand there. Uh, just I was a little slow. Um, but I I'm going to make the same comment that I've been making at every IETF. Um, the uh postquantum transition is mandated. We have limited time to get it done. um devices that are deployed as of 2030 effectively have to be PQC capable. Putting out an MTI draft at this point in time that actually standardizes algorithms that are about to be deprecated is counterintuitive. I understand why we're doing it, but I still think we shouldn't. So, I just have to put that out there on the record again. Yes, noted. >> Deb's just coming up to mic. One second. >> So, now that you have it submitted, you could probably start work on the next one, >> the update, right? Um, just guarant, you know, I mean, you're gonna need it's going to take a little bit of time to do that, right? So don't short change my reviews in terms of your ability to reply to them, but you should start working on that update now because now it's clear what you should do or it's more clear what you should do for this. Whereas before when you started this LMS and HMS were all you had. Now you have other options, right? So you just need to you need to choose wisely and you need to think about like the working group needs to think about it a little bit and decide what the best course is. But now you can start working on that update now that you have MTI at least in the queue, right? >> Yep. Like I said, I understand. I just wanted it on the record. >> All right. Uh so this draft uh suit trust domains has been through uh the IESG once now. Uh we've had uh a number of comments, a number of discusses. Um all of the discussed positions have been cleared by the IESG. Uh we did have one ballot comment about uh the title and introduction. So what's happening here is that there's a lot of work that's gone into specifically the dependencies component of suit trust domains. And so if you read through it, you sort of start to get the impression that this is really just about dependencies. Um the argument originally of course was that uh you only actually need dependencies if you have multiple trust domains. And so there's uh there's a couple of other scenarios and a couple of other things you might need even if you don't need dependencies uh when you have multiple trust domains. And so that that's sort of where this came from. Um, we've had a bit of a discussion recently about whether we should go through the process of renaming the draft and rewriting the introduction. The issue here is that this doesn't change the content except the introduction and the title. Um, but what it does do is brings it back from the IESG to the working group and then it has to go out again. Um, so this seems like pretty a pretty big overhead for a pretty small gain. Um, and the position um that I and at least one of the other contributors have is that uh this is more effort than it's worth and that it doesn't actually gain our impleers much. Um, so that being the case, uh, we are taking the view that this probably shouldn't have its title changed or its introduction rewritten even if a casual reader will say, "Hey, why isn't this just all about dependencies?" The answer is that well, it could have been, I suppose, but there are a few things that that aren't. And um what we are left with is uh a question as to how much more work we want to do just to change a title. Um so that being the case uh we we went round that loop once and have decided that we're going to leave it where it is. Um all of the other comments have been addressed. The only remaining uh comment that has come back from the IESG in the last couple of days is that it might be nice to expand the uh the acronym of CDDL and the other of TE on first use uh even though they are terms that are imported from referenced drafts. So um maybe we should issue an update for that. I'm not sure. Um, but that is a comment. It's not a discuss. So, it's not a blocker. >> Go ahead, Deb. >> No, the the editor will take care of that. They'll see that it's not expanded and it's not in the big broader already defined acronym list and they'll make you they'll make you do it. There's a couple of other typos in this draft that they will also make you fix, which is fine. I mean, that's why I didn't make you go back and reissue it again. just to like expand a couple of acronyms and fix a couple typos because the editor will do that. >> Okay. Um well, in that case then there are no substantive comments remaining. Uh all of the comments that there were have been replied to. Uh so I think we are in good shape in that regard. So what have we changed? Well, there's been a few changes uh since IETF122. Uh there's been explanations expanded. Uh one of the requests was that we add a summary of the different features that are implemented in this draft uh to the the introduction. Um there's also been there was a certain amount of terminology which was defined in the suit manifest and then carried over to this draft as well. And so I've removed those duplicate definitions especially because some of them uh actually have different content. So it's better to have a consistent definition in one place than two conflicting ones. Um there was a there's a section in this draft called required checks and the required checks are not ordered per se. They they don't actually say um which one has to happen first or anything like that. Um and this is a carryover as well from the original suit manifest. It doesn't have ordering on the required checks either. the there was a request to show how these required checks were integrated into the existing ones since this does update a list that's in the manifest. Um, so I went back and forth with the uh with the the reviewer on that a little bit and came to the conclusion that we should be able to treat it mostly as an unordered list that the order shouldn't actually be relevant and be implementation defined. Uh but there are certain exceptions to this where things do have to happen in a particular order and that is now called out explicitly in this draft saying what has to happen before something or after something. Um we there's a bunch more cross references that have been added uh and you know a dangling reference pruned um and the security considerations uh prior to this version of the draft were essentially go look at the suit manifest security considerations or or actually no more specifically go and look in uh the the information model because it contains a security model. So that wasn't really up to snuff. Um, and it's now got a pretty substantial security consideration section that applies specifically to the features that are added in this version of the draft. So in addition to the uh changes that I've described, these are the the residual changes since working group last call. just so that you've got an idea of uh where we came from when the working group agreed to this document and where we are now. So uh we had a a little bit in terms of terminology and improving that like adding a reference to the isolation level. Um changing manifest hash to being what it the actual name of the object in the suit manifest. Um, we've also added a normative reference to the suit firmware encryption draft uh that was needed specifically to be able to reference how to do encrypted manifests uh despite otherwise not really needing the firmware encryption draft in this draft. Um, some unused references have also been pruned and we've added uh references to things like CDDL and Seabore so that that can be expanded out uh in this draft to make it a little bit more standalone. And the examples also needed an update after a couple of changes got made. Uh I think that was predominantly to the ordering of the um elements in the uh manifest when we added in candidate verification. Um so that's that's what I've got for the suit trust domains. As far as I know, our next steps are that it goes back to the IESG. There are no further comments there. So, from what I understand, this should be headed for the editor's queue. Now, >> I'm I'm waiting for Deb to stand up and tell me I'm wrong. >> Deb's um already done that one today. >> Okay. >> Just just before. So, >> it's all good. >> It's approved. So, it's gone through. Back to ISG. Nove to go to editor's cube. There you go. Great. >> Um, Brendan, yeah, I I think I read your email about the comment on the update management. Yes, as far as I understand the status on uh suit update management at the moment um nothing has changed that much I know for sure. Um as far as I understand the next step on it which Deb mentioned earlier is for her to have a look at it and and then of course we will respond to her comments which there will inevitably be many um and uh we'll go from there. Okay. >> Okay. Um any other topics? Then I would like to show the um CD syntax check method on the G from the GitHub. >> Yeah. Mhm. Yeah. >> Nope. Um yes. So the meet echo um screen sharing is not working right now. So if you go to the GitHub with on the suit report, I added the com make file command saying um check uh CDD. May I read it? Yes, >> I'll just put the link in there. >> Okay. Yeah. Yeah. Yeah. We will add the comment in the um the link to the chat chat and yes, how to conduct CDD syntax check. So um Carsten Kabo he he added the new tool CDDLC which is uh different from CDDL. So inside the make file it um when you type make space dash C space CDDL um CDDL syntax check then it will um um execute the command CDDC with the um more readable uh um syntax check warning or error message back. And initially before I add this to the to this make file I was every time I was to conduct the thorough syntax check was um git cloning the tip protocol and then um use the um c CDDF file and the seat s suit report. So this is much easier and at the same time if you see the readme and the git repo uh GitHub it's it it's um how to when you modify the um um MD file or CDDF file what to do for the um in the repository to update and then also how to submit the draft. So anybody in in for use core or CO in the maintaining the draft in the GitHub and need to submit to the draft. Yes, please uh copy this uh readme and would like to have a any kind of um um feedback is welcome. Yes. Okay. That any other comment question? >> Okay. Well, if there's nothing else, I guess that that would mean that we could close this meeting off. Um, thank you so much for our note takers and we will hopefully be organizing an intra meeting soon which we will do in the mailing list. >> Thank you. >> Thank you. Hello. How are you? [Laughter] Okay, >> this
